sentence_reviews.text: A neuro-symbolic model combines learned continuous representations with symbolic rules, programs, constraints, knowledge graphs, or proof procedures.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning supports learned continuous representations; AIMA supports symbolic rules/logic/proofs; Sen et al. 2021 abstract discusses neuro-symbolic ILP and logical neural networks with first-order logic rules.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is defensible across neural and symbolic sources; knowledge graphs/proof procedures are broader than a single inspected source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Neuro-symbolic models is to connect Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution to downstream capabilities including Theorem proving & symbolic deduction, Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports logic, proof, and reasoning; Sen et al. 2021 supports neuro-symbolic logical neural networks. The packet lacks theorem-proving-specific model evidence.
sentence_reviews.notes: Logic/program components are supported; theorem proving and mathematical AI downstream claims are plausible but not directly established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Theorem proving & symbolic deduction
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA logic/proof material and neuro-symbolic ILP evidence support symbolic deduction generally.
sentence_reviews.notes: Symbolic deduction is supported; 'substrate for theorem proving' is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Automated theorem proving & mathematical AI
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA includes logic/proof topics; Sen et al. supports neuro-symbolic logic, but neither source directly demonstrates automated mathematical AI systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim should be narrowed or supported by theorem-proving-specific sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning, Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sources support neural learning and symbolic logic dependencies; no cited source directly states the generic limitation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependency clause is partly supported; limitation language is generic.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuro-symbolic models follows the learning setup expressed by Neuro-symbolic & physics-informed learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Sen et al. 2021 supports neuro-symbolic learning; PINO supports physics-informed learning as a separate strand.
sentence_reviews.notes: Neuro-symbolic learning is supported, but combining it with physics-informed learning as one core setup overstates this model node.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuro-symbolic models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Constraint propagation, logic & program execution.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports logic/constraints; Sen et al. 2021 describes first-order logic rules in neuro-symbolic ILP.
sentence_reviews.notes: Logic/programmatic symbolic components are supported; constraint propagation is broader than the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuro-symbolic models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA knowledge representation and Sen et al. logic rules support symbolic knowledge, but not knowledge graphs specifically.
sentence_reviews.notes: Symbolic knowledge is supported; knowledge graph/ontology/schema artifacts need more specific evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Neuro-symbolic models extends or specializes the architecture family Transformers.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet provides a transformer reference only as dependency background; no cited neuro-symbolic source requires transformers.
sentence_reviews.notes: Neuro-symbolic models can be transformer-based, but the cited evidence does not support this as a common specialization.

edge_reviews.notes: Neuro-symbolic learning is supported, but physics-informed learning is not inherent to the model node; core strength is overstated.
Symbolic rules and logic support the composes edge; core strength is reasonable for the combined node.
Symbolic knowledge representation is supported, but knowledge graphs/ontologies specifically are not directly evidenced; common strength is plausible.
No inspected source supports transformer specialization as a dependency for neuro-symbolic models in general.
